asi.hypothesis: Baseline captures current QuickJS-port parser test coverage before new parser experiments.
asi.note: parser_tests secondary metric parsed as 0 due autoresearch.sh summary extraction bug; fix script in next experiment before interpreting that secondary metric.

description: Port QuickJS numeric separator parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding numeric separator lexing and tests for QuickJS number literal cases should increase parser coverage while preserving existing behavior.
asi.learned: Lexer needed underscore-aware digit scanning and explicit legacy-leading-zero separator validation. Fixed autoresearch.sh parser_tests metric extraction in the same kept experiment.
asi.quickjs_cases: Covers valid 1_0 and invalid 0_0, 00_0, 01_0, 08_0, 09_0 from test_number_literals.

description: Port QuickJS regexp literal skip coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding lexer regexp-goal tracking and regexp literal AST support should parse QuickJS regexp-skip cases where `/` follows assignment operators.
asi.learned: A minimal previous-token regexp_allowed? heuristic is enough for current parser coverage; future work must refine lexical goals for division-heavy expressions.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports test_regexp_skip forms `[a, b = /abc\(/] = [1]` and `[a, b =/abc\(/] = [2]`.

asi.hypothesis: Adding whole-template tokenization and tagged-template postfix parsing should cover QuickJS template parser skip cases without full template AST semantics yet.
asi.learned: A scanner that skips nested `${...}` and nested backtick templates is enough for parser-level coverage of current QuickJS template tests; future semantic lowering will need real quasis/expressions.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports test_template plain/tagged template examples and test_template_skip nested template example.

asi.hypothesis: Adding array binding pattern AST support should cover QuickJS destructuring declaration syntax and prepare for arrow/function parameter patterns.
asi.learned: Parser declarations now branch through parse_binding_pattern; array patterns support holes, defaults, and rest elements without affecting array expression parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports test_destructuring `function * g () { return 0; }; var [x] = g();` and adds adjacent default/rest binding syntax coverage.

description: Port QuickJS arrow parameter syntax coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding parenthesized arrow-parameter detection plus object patterns and parameter defaults should cover QuickJS function-length syntax forms without implementing function length semantics.
asi.learned: A token scan for matching paren followed by `=>` cleanly distinguishes parenthesized arrow params from grouping for current coverage. Formal params now reuse binding patterns and assignment patterns.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports syntax shape from test_function_length: `(a,b=1,c)=>{}`, `([a,b])=>{}`, `({a,b})=>{}`, `(c,[a,b]=1,d)=>{}`.

description: Port QuickJS private field parse coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding minimal class body parsing and private identifiers should cover QuickJS parser regression where division after a private field must not be lexed as regexp.
asi.learned: Existing regexp_allowed? heuristic already treats identifier before slash as division context; adding `#` punctuator plus private member parsing lets the regression be expressed in parser tests.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports syntax from test_op1 class Q private field division case: `class Q { #x = 10; f() { return (this.#x / 2); } }`.

description: Port QuickJS new expression call chain coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding `new` expression parsing should let parser tests represent the full QuickJS private-field regression assertion shape rather than only the class body.
asi.learned: Prefix `new` parsing composes with existing postfix call/member parsing, producing `new Q().f()` as a NewExpression wrapped by member/call nodes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Extends private-field regression coverage with `assert(new Q().f(), 5);` call-chain syntax.

description: Port QuickJS try catch parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding try/catch/finally statement parsing should cover QuickJS constructor/delete parser shapes and enable more syntax-test ports.
asi.learned: TryStatement can reuse existing block and binding-pattern parsing; optional catch binding and finally block compose without touching expression parser.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports try/catch forms used by test_constructor and test_delete: `try { new G() } catch (ex_) { ... }` and catch/finally syntax.

asi.hypothesis: Adding switch/case/default statement parsing should cover QuickJS assert_throws helper syntax and broaden statement parser coverage.
asi.learned: Switch consequent parsing needs a statement-list variant that stops at `case`, `default`, or `}`; existing statement parser can otherwise be reused.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports assert_throws switch over `typeof func` with `case 'string'`, `case 'function'`, default, and break statements.

description: Port QuickJS optional chaining parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding optional chaining postfix parsing should cover QuickJS optional-chaining syntactic forms and expose unary/postfix precedence issues.
asi.learned: Lexer needs `?.` punctuator. Unary parsing must let postfix tails bind to its operand so `delete a?.b` becomes delete of the optional member expression rather than optional member of `(delete a)`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports optional chaining syntax forms `a?.b`, `a?.b()`, `a?.["b"]()`, and `delete a?.b["c"]`.

description: Port QuickJS throw statement parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding throw statement parsing and its line-terminator restricted production should cover QuickJS assert helper syntax and an important ASI rule.
asi.learned: Throw is a statement, not a prefix expression; parser now emits a dedicated ThrowStatement and reports a targeted error when a line terminator follows `throw`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports assert helper `throw Error(...)` syntax and QuickJS syntax coverage for throw line-terminator errors.

description: Port QuickJS sequence expression parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Representing comma operators as SequenceExpression should improve AST fidelity for QuickJS template and expression syntax coverage.
asi.learned: The existing Pratt comma precedence could be preserved; only binary node construction needed special handling to flatten comma chains.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports comma sequence syntax used by test_template expression `${a, b}` via direct parser coverage of `(a, b, c)`.

description: Port QuickJS class feature parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Enhancing class parsing for static modifiers, accessors, extends, and class expressions should cover a substantial QuickJS class syntax slice without changing runtime semantics.
asi.learned: `static()` must remain a method named static, while `static F()` and `static x = ...` use a modifier; accessor parsing reuses class key/formal parameter/block helpers.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports test_class syntax for static methods, getters, extends, super member calls, static fields, and `var E1 = class E { ... }` class expressions.

asi.hypothesis: Existing function/arrow/default-parameter support should be validated against QuickJS argument-scope syntax, and autoresearch notes should be refreshed to avoid repeating completed candidates.
asi.learned: Current parser already handles the argument-scope syntax slice after prior arrow/default/class work; added non-redundant regression coverage and updated autoresearch.md with completed areas and next candidates.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports test_argument_scope syntax forms with function default parameters, arrow defaults, eval calls, named function expressions, and nested arrow default parameters.

description: Port QuickJS reserved binding name coverage

asi.hypothesis: Classifying future reserved words as keywords and tightening binding identifier acceptance should cover QuickJS reserved-name parser errors while preserving await contextual binding allowance.
asi.learned: Adding implements/interface/package/private/protected/public to keyword lexing makes declaration binding validation catch them uniformly; `await` remains a contextual binding name in script-mode parser coverage.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports test_reserved_names coverage for yield, implements, interface, let, package, private, protected, public, static as invalid var bindings and await as allowed.

description: Port QuickJS template destructuring parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Exact QuickJS template-skip destructuring declaration should now be representable by the combined object-pattern and nested-template parser support; adding regression coverage verifies the composition.
asi.learned: No parser change was needed: object binding default initializers compose with whole-template tokenization and object expression initializers.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports exact shape from test_template_skip: `var { b = `${a + `a${a}` }baz` } = {};`.

description: Port QuickJS function expression name parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing function expression and parenthesized callee parsing should cover QuickJS function-expression-name and IIFE syntax; adding tests verifies composition without parser changes.
asi.learned: FunctionExpression IDs, parenthesized function expressions, and parenthesized arrow functions already compose with postfix call parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports representative syntax from test_function_expr_name: named function expression assignment plus function and arrow IIFEs.

description: Port QuickJS super call parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing class and call/member parsing should cover super constructor calls and super member calls from QuickJS class tests; adding explicit coverage guards that composition.
asi.learned: No parser changes needed: `super` is represented as an Identifier and composes with CallExpression and MemberExpression nodes inside class methods.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports class D syntax from test_class: `super(); this.z = 20;`, `return super.f();`, and `return super["F"]();`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing member-call and object-literal parsing should cover QuickJS prototype descriptor syntax; adding coverage prevents regressions in nested member chains and object literal booleans.
asi.learned: No parser change needed: nested member expressions, CallExpression arguments, FunctionExpression initializers, and boolean object properties compose correctly.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports representative test_prototype syntax: `Object.defineProperty(g, "prototype", { writable: false })` and `f.prototype.constructor` assertion call.

asi.hypothesis: Adding for/for-in/for-of parsing broadens statement support needed for JavaScript/QuickJS compatibility without changing runtime behavior.
asi.learned: Classic for loops can reuse expression parsing, but for-in/of needs a small no-in fast path for simple identifiers before parsing the initializer as a binary `in` expression.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible parser coverage for `for (var i=...; ...; i++)`, `for (x in obj)`, and `for (let x of xs)` forms.

asi.hypothesis: Adding continue statement parsing complements existing break/label/loop support and improves JavaScript statement compatibility.
asi.learned: Continue parsing mirrors break parsing, including optional labels and ASI line-terminator handling via existing token metadata.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `continue`, labeled `continue loop`, and composition with labeled while and for loops.

asi.hypothesis: Adding static import/export parsing expands parser compatibility beyond script-only QuickJS language tests while preserving hand-written parser architecture.
asi.learned: `from`/`as` are contextual identifiers rather than keywords in the lexer; module parser should check identifier values instead of keyword tokens.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible ES module syntax coverage for side-effect imports, default/named/namespace imports, named re-exports, and export declarations.

asi.hypothesis: Adding object spread parsing complements existing array spread and object literal support without affecting property parsing.
asi.learned: Object literal parser can emit the existing SpreadElement node for `...expr` before falling through to accessor/regular property parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible object spread literal coverage for `{ a: 1, ...rest, b }`, extending spread support beyond array literals.

asi.hypothesis: Adding async arrow recognition should handle JavaScript async parameter syntax and preserve line-terminator sensitivity after `async`.
asi.learned: Async arrow detection can reuse the existing parenthesized-arrow lookahead after temporarily advancing past `async`; single-parameter async arrows need a separate two-token lookahead.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `async x => await x` and `async (a, b = 1) => { return await a; }`.

description: Port QuickJS syntax error parser coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing error-tolerant parser paths should report errors for a representative subset of QuickJS syntax-error tests; adding assertions verifies no silent ok results for incomplete constructs.
asi.learned: Current parser already returns error tuples for incomplete do/if/while/class/switch/try forms; no implementation change needed for this coverage slice.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports representative test_syntax error inputs including `do`, `do;`, `do{}`, `if`, `if 1`, `if ;`, `while`, `class`, `switch`, and `try {}`.

asi.hypothesis: Adding export default parsing completes a common ES module declaration shape missing from the previous module syntax pass.
asi.learned: Default export can dispatch to existing function/class declaration parsing or expression parsing with semicolon consumption for expression defaults.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `export default function`, `export default class`, and `export default expression` module forms.

asi.hypothesis: Treating `import` as an identifier only in call position should add dynamic import expression coverage without impacting static import declarations.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure check failed in known flaky BeamProcess monitor timing test (`noproc` vs `kaboom`), so autoresearch rules require checks_failed despite focused parser benchmark passing.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the small dynamic import change and rerun; if the same unrelated check flakes, rerun checks/focused web APIs before deciding whether to retry the experiment.

asi.hypothesis: Treating `import` as an identifier only when followed by `(` should support dynamic import expressions while leaving static import declarations statement-level.
asi.learned: The change is safely localized to prefix parsing; static `import ...` is still captured earlier by statement parsing, while expression `import("mod")` becomes a normal CallExpression.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible dynamic import expression coverage for `value = import("mod");`.

asi.hypothesis: Adding computed property keys to object literal parsing should improve compatibility with dynamic property names and object methods.
asi.learned: A `parse_property_key_with_computed/1` helper lets regular properties and methods carry a computed flag while preserving existing parse_property_key callers for imports/exports/classes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for object literal computed property and computed method syntax: `{ [name]: 1, [method]() { ... } }`.

asi.hypothesis: Adding a const-declaration initializer check improves syntax diagnostics without changing the parser AST shape for valid declarations.
asi.learned: Validation can run after declarator parsing and before semicolon consumption; it reports an error while preserving the partial VariableDeclaration AST.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for invalid `const value;` and valid `const value = 1;` declaration syntax.

asi.hypothesis: Adding DebuggerStatement parsing should cover a simple JavaScript statement form with negligible parser risk.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks failed in known flaky BeamProcess monitor timing test (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`), requiring checks_failed and auto-revert.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the DebuggerStatement AST/parser/test change and rerun; likely keep if web_apis check does not hit the unrelated timing flake.

asi.hypothesis: Adding DebuggerStatement parsing should cover a simple JavaScript statement form with negligible parser risk.
asi.learned: Debugger parsing is a statement-level keyword case that can consume an optional semicolon using the existing ASI helper.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible parser coverage for `debugger;`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing async-function and generator parsing should compose for async generator declarations and expressions; explicit coverage guards that combination.
asi.learned: No implementation change required: `consume_async_modifier/1` followed by `consume_generator_marker/1` handles `async function *` in both declarations and expressions.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `async function *g() { ... }` and `async function *h() { ... }` expression syntax.

asi.hypothesis: Parsing optional `await` after `for` should support async iteration syntax while reusing existing ForOfStatement AST shape.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks failed in known Process.monitor timing flake (`noproc` instead of expected exit reason), so rules require checks_failed and revert.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply for-await-of parser/test changes and rerun; consider running the focused flaky web API test separately only for diagnosis, not as a substitute for autoresearch checks.

asi.hypothesis: Parsing optional `await` after `for` should support async iteration syntax while reusing existing ForOfStatement AST shape.
asi.learned: The existing for-of parser only needs to carry an `await?` flag from the `for await (` prefix into ForOfStatement; classic for and for-in remain unchanged structurally.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `for await (const value of iterable) { await value; }` inside an async function.

asi.hypothesis: Adding export-all module declarations expands ES module coverage beyond named/default exports without touching script parsing.
asi.learned: `as` is another contextual identifier; export-all parsing must inspect the current token value rather than lexer keyword type.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `export * from "dep";` and `export * as ns from "dep2";`.

asi.hypothesis: Adding import.meta as a prefix meta-property handles another ES module-only expression without conflating it with dynamic import calls.
asi.learned: `import` now has two expression-level special cases: `import(` stays a call callee and `import.` creates a MetaProperty that can continue through normal member parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module expression coverage for `url = import.meta.url;`.

description: Attempt QuickJS operator and update syntax coverage

asi.hypothesis: Porting QuickJS operator/update syntax should verify existing precedence handling and fix prefix update parsing for member/index operands.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks timed out without output after the focused parser benchmark passed, so autoresearch rules require checks_failed and auto-revert.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the small prefix-update/member-tail fix and QuickJS operator syntax test, then rerun; the parser change is localized to prefix update parsing.

description: Retry QuickJS operator and update syntax coverage

asi.hypothesis: Reapplying the prefix-update/member-tail fix should keep parser coverage passing and allow backpressure checks to recover after a timeout.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks timed out again with no captured output, so the experiment cannot be kept under autoresearch rules.
asi.next_action_hint: Defer this operator/update fix or investigate why `autoresearch.checks.sh` is timing out before retrying; try a no-op checks run from a clean tree to distinguish environment flake from parser changes.

description: Port QuickJS constructor and unary syntax coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing parser support for `new`, `delete`, and `typeof` should cover QuickJS constructor/delete/type queries from test_language without new implementation risk.
asi.learned: No parser changes were required for `new Object`, `new F(2)`, `delete a.x`, or `typeof unknown_var`; current prefix/new/member composition already handles them.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_op2`/`test_delete` syntax shapes for constructor calls with and without arguments, delete of a member expression, and typeof of an identifier.

description: Port QuickJS binary operator syntax coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing Pratt precedence and update-expression parsing should cover representative QuickJS arithmetic, exponentiation, postfix member updates, `in`, `instanceof`, and logical syntax.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for binary/logical precedence or postfix member/index updates; prefix update of member/index operands remains a deferred parser-correctness issue from failed attempts.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_op1`, `test_inc_dec`, and `test_op2` syntax shapes for exponentiation precedence, postfix updates, `in`, `instanceof`, and `&&`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing formal-parameter parsing should already cover QuickJS function-length syntax involving array/object patterns and defaulted pattern parameters.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for arrow parameters using array patterns, object patterns, or defaulted array patterns.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_function_length` syntax shapes for `([a,b]) => {}`, `({a,b}) => {}`, and `(c, [a,b] = 1, d) => {}`.

description: Attempt QuickJS invalid number literal coverage

asi.hypothesis: Porting QuickJS invalid number literal diagnostics should catch `0.a` and numeric-separator errors while preserving valid `0.` syntax.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks timed out immediately with no output, so autoresearch rules require checks_failed and auto-revert before switching tasks.
asi.next_action_hint: If resuming parser coverage later, reapply the localized lexer validation for dotted numeric literals followed by identifiers and split tests into focused files first.

description: Port QuickJS dotted number literal coverage

asi.hypothesis: QuickJS rejects `0.a` while accepting `0.`; adding a lexer-side check for dotted number literals followed by an identifier improves numeric literal diagnostics without changing valid tokenization.
asi.learned: The lexer already has the next byte available after number scanning, so validation can reject only the ambiguous `number.` + identifier case and preserve standalone `0.`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_number_literals` syntax coverage for invalid `0.a` and keeps valid `0.;` as a guard.

description: Attempt inline QuickJS prefix update coverage

asi.hypothesis: Adding prefix update member parsing to the existing binary operator test should improve QuickJS update-expression compatibility.
asi.rollback_reason: Focused tests and checks passed, but the primary metric did not improve because the coverage was folded into an existing QuickJS test rather than a distinct tracked coverage item.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the parser fix with a separate meaningful QuickJS prefix-update test so the coverage metric reflects the new case without redundant assertions.

description: Port QuickJS prefix update member coverage

asi.hypothesis: Prefix update parsing should accept member and computed-member operands just like postfix updates in QuickJS syntax.
asi.learned: Prefix update parsing must allow postfix member/index tails on the prefix operand before wrapping it in UpdateExpression; otherwise `++a[0]` parses as member access on `++a`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_inc_dec` syntax shapes for prefix update of object members and array elements: `++a.x` and `--a[0]`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing delete/member parsing should cover QuickJS delete edge syntax for computed string indexes and super-member deletion inside methods.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; unary delete already allows member tails on its operand, including literal computed members and `super` member expressions inside object methods.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_delete` syntax shapes for `delete "abc"[100]` and `delete super.a` inside an object method.

description: Port QuickJS arguments object syntax coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing member/call parsing should cover QuickJS `arguments` object syntax and ordinary function calls from test_arguments.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for `arguments.length`, `arguments[0]`, bare `arguments`, `gc()`, or calls with numeric arguments.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_arguments` syntax shapes for `arguments.length`, indexed `arguments`, bare `arguments`, `gc()`, and `f2(1, 3)`/`f3(0)` calls.

description: Port QuickJS contextual class field coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing class-member parsing should handle QuickJS contextual `get`, `set`, `async`, and `static` class element syntax without treating every contextual word as a modifier.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; class fields named `get`/`set`/`async`, arrow-valued fields, and a method literally named `static` already parse distinctly from accessors/static modifiers.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_class` syntax shapes for class P: `get;`, `set;`, `async;`, arrow-valued class fields, and `static() { ... }` as a non-static method.

description: Port QuickJS if syntax error coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing parser diagnostics should reject QuickJS `if` forms that omit required parenthesized conditions across literals, regexp/template literals, identifiers, and unicode escapes.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `parse_if_statement/1` already expects `(` and reports syntax errors for these QuickJS `test_syntax` inputs.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports additional QuickJS `test_syntax` invalid inputs: `if 'abc'`, `if `abc``, `if /abc/`, `if abcd`, `if abc\\u0064`, and `if \\u0123`.

asi.hypothesis: Supporting shorthand default properties inside object assignment patterns expands destructuring coverage beyond declarations while staying within the existing AST shape.
asi.learned: Object property parsing can represent `b = 1` as a shorthand property whose value is an AssignmentPattern; this fixes `({ a, b = 1 } = obj)` without changing declaration binding-pattern parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible object destructuring assignment coverage for `({ a, b = 1 } = obj);`, extending the QuickJS destructuring/template-skip coverage area.

description: Port array destructuring assignment rest coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing array spread parsing should cover the syntax shape used by rest elements in array destructuring assignment contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `[a, ...rest] = value` is represented with the existing ArrayExpression and SpreadElement nodes in assignment-left position.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible array destructuring assignment coverage for `[a, ...rest] = value;`, complementing the existing QuickJS array binding and spread coverage.

description: Port QuickJS static this field coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing class field initializer parsing should cover QuickJS static field initializers that reference `this` through member expressions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `this` is represented as an Identifier in expression position and composes with member parsing inside static field initializers.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_class` syntax shape `class S { static z = this.x; }`.

description: Port QuickJS grouped optional call coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing parenthesized-expression, optional-member, call, and member-tail parsing should compose for QuickJS grouped optional call chains.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for `(a?.b)().c` or `(a?.["b"])().c`; optional member expressions continue through call/member tails after grouping.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_optional_chaining` syntax shapes `(a?.b)().c` and `(a?.["b"])().c`.

description: Port QuickJS number member call coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing parenthesized literal and member-call parsing should cover QuickJS numeric conversion syntax using a large number followed by `.toString()`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for parenthesized number member calls; the parser preserves the large integer literal and composes member/call tails correctly.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_cvt` syntax shape `(19686109595169230000).toString()`.

description: Port QuickJS boxed constructor syntax coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing `new` and equality parsing should cover QuickJS boxed primitive constructor expressions in comparison contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for parenthesized `new Number(...)`/`new String(...)` expressions on either side of equality operators.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_eq` syntax shapes `(new Number(1)) == 1`, `2 == (new Number(2))`, and `(new String("abc")) == "abc"`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing generator, `new`, and try/catch parsing should cover QuickJS generator-constructor TypeError syntax setup.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks failed in the known flaky BeamProcess monitor timing test (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`), so autoresearch rules require checks_failed and auto-revert.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the generator constructor syntax test and rerun; no parser implementation change was involved, so a clean retry should keep if web_apis does not hit the unrelated flake.

asi.hypothesis: Existing generator, `new`, and try/catch parsing should cover QuickJS generator-constructor TypeError syntax setup.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for `function *G() {}`, `let ex;`, or `try { new G(); } catch (ex_) { ... }`; the retry passed after the unrelated web API flake cleared.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_constructor` syntax setup for attempting `new G()` on a generator inside try/catch.

description: Port QuickJS numeric conversion expression coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing binary/unary precedence should cover QuickJS numeric conversion syntax involving identifiers, strings, parenthesized unary expressions, multiplication/subtraction, bitwise OR, and unsigned shift.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; the Pratt parser produces the expected top-level bitwise/shift expressions and preserves nested arithmetic precedence.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_cvt` syntax shapes such as `NaN | 0`, `Infinity | 0`, `(-Infinity) | 0`, string `>>> 0`, and `(4294967296 * 3 - 4) >>> 0`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing unary parsing should cover QuickJS `void 0` syntax used in destructuring result assertions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `void` is already part of the unary operator set and composes with numeric literals.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_destructuring` syntax shape `void 0`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing module parser support should cover common default-only imports, named-only imports, and local named exports in addition to the previously covered mixed/re-export forms.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; contextual `from` handling and import/export specifier parsing already handle these module clause variants.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible ES module syntax coverage for `import defaultExport from`, `import { foo } from`, and `export { foo };`.

asi.hypothesis: Adding `new.target` as a meta-property mirrors the existing `import.meta` handling and improves function meta-property syntax coverage.
asi.learned: `new` needs a prefix special case before normal NewExpression parsing when followed by `.`, producing MetaProperty instead of trying to parse a constructor callee.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible meta-property coverage for `function f() { return new.target; }`.

description: Port QuickJS arrow length member coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing arrow-function grouping and member-tail parsing should cover QuickJS function-length assertions that access `.length` on parenthesized arrow functions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; grouped ArrowFunctionExpression values compose with member access for both default-parameter and destructuring-parameter cases.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_function_length` syntax shapes `((a, b = 1, c) => {}).length` and `(({a,b}) => {}).length`.

description: Port QuickJS class descriptor chain coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing call/member chain parsing should cover the long QuickJS class getter descriptor assertion syntax.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for nested member arguments and chained call/member/property access through `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(...).get.name`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_class` syntax shape `Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(C.prototype, "y").get.name === "get y"`.

asi.hypothesis: Class element parsing should preserve computed keys and support computed getters instead of losing the computed flag or treating `get [x]()` as a field named get.
asi.learned: Class keys now reuse the computed-property helper; accessor detection must treat `get [`/`set [` as accessor starts in addition to identifier keys.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible computed class element coverage for `[method]() {}`, `static [field] = 1`, and `get [value]() { ... }`.

asi.hypothesis: Object literal accessor parsing should support computed keys just like class accessors and regular object properties.
asi.learned: Object accessor detection must recognize `get [`/`set [` starts, and accessor property creation should carry the computed flag from the shared property-key helper.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible computed object accessor coverage for `get [name]()` and `set [name](value)`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing unary and relational operator parsing should cover additional QuickJS operator syntax from test_op1 without implementation changes.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for bitwise-not, logical-not, numeric relational, or string relational expressions in assignment/parenthesized contexts.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_op1` syntax shapes `~1`, `!1`, `(1 < 2)`, `(2 > 1)`, and `('b' > 'a')`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing equality parsing should cover QuickJS primitive equality and inequality syntax across null, undefined, booleans, strings, numbers, and object literals in expression contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; object-literal inequality needs expression grouping when used as a standalone expression statement, matching JavaScript's block-vs-object ambiguity.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_eq` syntax shapes for `null == undefined`, primitive equality comparisons, string/number comparisons, and grouped object-literal inequality.

asi.hypothesis: Object and class method parsing should distinguish `async m()`/`async [m]()` methods from fields named `async`, preserving async flags on the FunctionExpression.
asi.learned: An async-method lookahead handles identifier and computed method keys without affecting `async;` or `async = ...` fields because it only triggers when the key is followed by a parameter list.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible async object/class method coverage for `async m()`, `async [name]()`, and `static async [name]()`.

description: Port QuickJS async generator method coverage

asi.hypothesis: Async method parsing should also accept generator markers so object/class `async *m()` syntax composes with existing generator function AST fields.
asi.learned: The async-method parser can reuse `consume_generator_marker/1` after consuming `async`; lookahead must recognize `async *name(` as an async method start.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible async generator method coverage for object and class `async *m() { yield await x; }`.

asi.hypothesis: Object and class member parsing should accept generator method syntax and avoid parser recovery loops on leading `*` class/object elements.
asi.learned: Explicit generator-method branches for object and class elements parse `*m()` and computed `*[name]()` keys cleanly, preserving FunctionExpression.generator.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible generator method coverage for object `*m()`, class `*m()`, and static computed class generator `static *[name]()`.

description: Port rest parameter and spread call coverage

asi.hypothesis: Formal parameter and argument-list parsing should support rest/spread syntax using existing RestElement and SpreadElement AST nodes.
asi.learned: Adding explicit `...` branches to parameter and argument parsers enables rest parameters and spread call arguments without changing expression parsing elsewhere.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible coverage for `function f(...args)`, `f(1, ...args)`, and `((...args) => args)(...[1, 2])`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing try/catch parsing should already support optional catch binding and catch/finally combinations.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `parse_catch_clause/1` returns nil params when no parenthesized binding is present and composes with finally blocks.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible control-flow coverage for `try {} catch {}` and `try {} catch (e) {} finally {}`.

description: Port QuickJS array spread elision coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing array spread and elision parsing should cover QuickJS spread over an array literal hole.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; array elisions are represented as nil elements and compose inside SpreadElement arguments.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_spread` syntax shape `x = [ ...[ , ] ];`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing module export and async function parsing should compose for named and default async function exports, including async generators.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; export declaration dispatch already uses `function_start?/1`, which recognizes `async function` before parsing function declarations.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export async function`, `export default async function`, and `export default async function *`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing object binding pattern parsing should cover object rest properties in declarations and function parameters.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; object pattern parsing already emits RestElement for `...rest` and composes inside formal parameter parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible destructuring coverage for `var { a, ...rest } = obj` and `function f({ a, ...rest }) {}`.

asi.hypothesis: Object binding patterns should preserve computed keys just like object literals and class elements.
asi.learned: Object pattern parsing can reuse `parse_property_key_with_computed/1` and set the Property.computed flag while preserving shorthand/default behavior.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible destructuring coverage for computed object binding keys: `var { [key]: value } = obj` and defaulted computed parameters.

asi.hypothesis: Existing optional-chain parsing should handle optional calls on identifiers, member expressions, and optional-member chains.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `parse_optional_chain_tail/2` already supports `?.(` after any postfix expression and preserves optional flags on CallExpression.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible optional chaining coverage for `fn?.()`, `obj.method?.()`, and `obj?.method?.(x)`.

asi.hypothesis: Default export expression parsing should cover async and parenthesized arrow functions without parser changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks hit the known flaky BeamProcess monitor timing failure (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`), so autoresearch rules require checks_failed and auto-revert.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the default arrow export test and rerun; no parser implementation change was involved.

asi.hypothesis: Default export expression parsing should cover async and parenthesized arrow functions without parser changes.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `parse_export_default_declaration/1` falls through to expression parsing, which already recognizes async and parenthesized arrows.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export default async x => x` and `export default (x) => x`.

asi.hypothesis: Export specifier parsing should treat `default` as a contextual property key so default re-export forms parse without lexer changes.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `parse_property_key/1` accepts keyword tokens as Identifier keys, so `default as foo` and `foo as default` both work.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export { default as foo } from "dep"` and `export { foo as default }`.

asi.hypothesis: Named import specifier parsing should also accept `default` as a contextual imported binding name.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; named import parsing shares property-key handling with exports and accepts keyword tokens for imported names.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `import { default as foo } from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: The lexer should accept non-ASCII identifier characters so QuickJS-compatible Unicode identifier syntax can parse at least for direct UTF-8 source characters.
asi.learned: Allowing codepoints above ASCII in identifier start/part handling enables UTF-8 identifiers like `ȣ`; full Unicode escape identifier support remains a separate, more precise lexer task.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible Unicode identifier coverage for `var ȣ = 1;`, related to QuickJS `test_syntax` Unicode identifier inputs.

asi.hypothesis: The lexer should decode `\uXXXX` escapes in identifiers so QuickJS-compatible escaped identifier names parse to their cooked identifier value.
asi.learned: Identifier scanning now accumulates cooked parts and decodes simple four-digit Unicode escapes while preserving raw token text for diagnostics.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible Unicode escape identifier coverage for `var abc\u0064 = 1;`, related to QuickJS `test_syntax` escaped identifier inputs.

description: Port braced unicode escape identifier coverage

asi.hypothesis: Identifier escape decoding should also handle ES6 braced Unicode escapes so parser coverage is not limited to four-digit `\uXXXX` forms.
asi.learned: A small braced escape scanner can decode `\u{...}` identifier parts and continue the same cooked identifier accumulation path.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible braced Unicode escape identifier coverage for `var smile\u{79} = 1;`.

asi.hypothesis: Unicode escape identifier support should reject invalid escape forms and invalid codepoints instead of crashing or silently accepting malformed identifiers.
asi.learned: Identifier escape decoding now guards against surrogate/out-of-range codepoints and non-hex escape payloads, returning parser errors through the lexer error path.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible invalid Unicode identifier escape coverage for out-of-range, surrogate, and malformed `\u` identifier escapes.

asi.hypothesis: Unicode escape identifier decoding should also work after private-name markers in class fields and private member expressions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after lexer escape support; private-name parsing consumes the decoded identifier token after `#` in both field keys and member properties.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible private identifier escape coverage for `#\u0061` declarations and `this.#\u0061` access.

asi.hypothesis: Existing number lexing and member parsing should cover QuickJS number literal property access forms except the deliberately invalid `0.a` case.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; decimal, hex, binary, and octal numeric literals all compose with member access when the numeric token is complete before the dot.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_number_literals` valid member syntax shapes `0.1.a`, `0x1.a`, `0b1.a`, and `0o1.a`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing call parsing should cover QuickJS parseInt/parseFloat number-literal test helpers with string and radix arguments.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; ordinary call expressions preserve string literal arguments containing numeric separators and Infinity-like text.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_number_literals` call syntax shapes for `parseInt("0_1")`, `parseInt("1_0", 8)`, and `parseFloat("Infinity.")`/`parseFloat("Infinity_")`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing call/member tail parsing should cover QuickJS argument-scope call shapes with method calls and repeated call/index tails.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; nested call expressions continue through computed member and property member tails correctly.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_argument_scope` syntax shapes such as `f.call(123)`, `f(12)()[0]`, and further member access on nested calls.

asi.hypothesis: Existing string literal expression statement parsing should cover QuickJS directive prologue syntax at program and function-body positions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; directive prologues are currently represented as Literal expression statements, while full strict-mode semantic validation remains separate.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_reserved_names` syntax shape for top-level and function-body `"use strict";` directives.

asi.hypothesis: String literal scanning should decode common JavaScript hex and Unicode escapes instead of treating `x`/`u` as literal escaped characters.
asi.learned: String escape scanning now handles `\xNN`, `\uNNNN`, and `\u{...}` escapes with codepoint validation, sharing the same validity ranges as identifier escapes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible string literal escape coverage for `"\x61\u0062\u{63}"` producing `abc`.

asi.hypothesis: String escape scanner should surface diagnostics for malformed hex/unicode escapes and invalid codepoints.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure checks hit the known flaky BeamProcess monitor timing failure (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`), so autoresearch rules require checks_failed and auto-revert.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the invalid string escape diagnostics test and rerun; implementation already passed focused parser tests and failure was unrelated.

asi.hypothesis: String escape scanner should surface diagnostics for malformed hex/unicode escapes and invalid codepoints.
asi.learned: Malformed `\x`, fixed `\u`, and braced `\u{...}` string escapes now flow through lexer errors and parser error tuples without crashing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible invalid string escape coverage for malformed `\x`, malformed `\u`, and out-of-range braced Unicode string escapes.

description: Port QuickJS object names call-chain coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing call/member tail parsing should cover QuickJS spread-test helper syntax that chains `Object.getOwnPropertyNames(x).toString()`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; nested member calls continue through an additional member-call tail after the first call expression.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_spread` syntax shape `Object.getOwnPropertyNames(x).toString()`.

description: Port QuickJS class instance call-chain coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing `new` expression and member-call tail parsing should cover QuickJS class instance method calls, including contextual property names.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `new P()` composes with member call tails for ordinary, contextual, and `static` property names.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_class` syntax shapes `new P().get()`, `.set()`, `.async()`, and `.static()`.

description: Port QuickJS static class call-chain coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing member-call parsing should cover QuickJS static class method call assertions from `test_class`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; identifier member call chains with empty argument lists cover static class method calls and class-expression name-scope calls.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_class` syntax shapes `C.F()`, `D.F()`, `D.G()`, `D.H()`, and `E1.F()`.

description: Port QuickJS strict equality logical coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing Pratt precedence should cover QuickJS increment/decrement assertions that combine strict equality comparisons with logical AND.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; strict equality binds tighter than `&&`, and member/index expressions compose on the left side of strict equality.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_inc_dec` syntax shapes like `r === 1 && a === 2` and member/index strict-equality chains.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp lexical-goal handling should accept regexp literals after `return` and allow member-call tails on regexp literals.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; regexp tokens carry flags and compose with member/call postfix parsing after literal scanning.
asi.quickjs_cases: Extends QuickJS regexp literal coverage with `return /abc/g` and `/abc/.test(value)` syntax shapes.

asi.hypothesis: Existing switch, unary, throw, and call parsing should cover QuickJS helper dispatch syntax using `switch (typeof func)`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; switch discriminants accept unary expressions and case/default consequents compose with break and throw statements.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS helper syntax shape `switch (typeof func) { case "function": break; default: throw Error("bad"); }`.

description: Port QuickJS catch instanceof assignment coverage

asi.hypothesis: Existing try/catch, delete, assignment, and instanceof parsing should cover QuickJS delete-error handling syntax.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; catch bodies parse assignment expressions whose right-hand side is a parenthesized `instanceof` binary expression.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_delete` syntax shape `try { delete null.a; } catch(e) { err = (e instanceof TypeError); }`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing function, `this`, member, and assignment parsing should cover QuickJS constructor function syntax.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `this` is represented as an identifier and composes with member assignment in function bodies.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS constructor helper syntax `function F(x) { this.x = x; }`.

description: Port QuickJS contextual property access coverage

asi.hypothesis: Object literal and property-member parsing should handle contextual keyword property names from QuickJS object operator tests.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; property keys and property identifiers accept keyword tokens such as `if` and `async` where JavaScript treats them as property names.
asi.quickjs_cases: Ports QuickJS `test_op2` syntax shapes `{ x: 1, if: 2, async: 3 }`, `a.if === 2`, and `a.async === 3`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing yield-expression parsing should cover delegated and bare yield forms in generator bodies.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `parse_yield_expression/1` already recognizes `yield *expr` and ASI/bare-yield endings.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible generator coverage for `yield *iterable;` and bare `yield;`.

asi.hypothesis: Await parsing should include postfix call/member tails so module-level `await import("dep")` parses as await of the dynamic import call rather than a call of an await expression.
asi.learned: `parse_await_expression/1` needed the same postfix-tail handling as unary expressions to bind calls/members to the awaited operand.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for top-level `await import("dep")` and `value = await promise`.

asi.hypothesis: The await postfix-tail fix should also cover ordinary awaited method calls and member access inside async functions.
asi.learned: No additional implementation change was required after the await binding fix; await now wraps CallExpression and MemberExpression operands correctly.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible async-function coverage for `await obj.method(arg)` and `await obj.value`.

asi.hypothesis: Default export parsing should allow anonymous function and class declarations, which are valid module syntax even though ordinary declarations require names.
asi.learned: Function and class declaration parsers now take a `require_name?` flag; default export dispatch uses optional-name parsing while normal statement/export declarations keep requiring names.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for anonymous `export default function()`, `export default async function()`, and `export default class {}`.

description: Port anonymous default generator export coverage

asi.hypothesis: The optional-name default export path should also compose with generator and async-generator function declarations.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after optional default function names; generator markers are already consumed before the optional identifier check.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for anonymous `export default function*()` and `export default async function*()`.

asi.hypothesis: Adding class static block parsing expands QuickJS-compatible class syntax coverage with a focused AST node and should not affect existing static fields/methods.
asi.learned: Class element parsing can distinguish `static {` before normal static member parsing and reuse block parsing to populate a StaticBlock AST node.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class static initialization block coverage for `class C { static { this.value = 1; } }`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing for-in/for-of parsing should compose with array and object binding patterns in loop variable declarations.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; for-loop declaration parsing uses the same declarator and binding-pattern paths as variable declarations.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible loop coverage for `for (const [key, value] of entries)` and `for (let { name } in objects)`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing Pratt operator tables should cover modern QuickJS-compatible nullish coalescing and logical assignment syntax.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `??` is represented as LogicalExpression and `??=`/`||=`/`&&=` as right-associative AssignmentExpression operators.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible modern expression coverage for `??`, `??=`, `||=`, and `&&=`.

asi.hypothesis: Lexer number scanning should keep `n` suffixes on integer literals as a single token and reject decimal bigint forms.
asi.learned: Number scanning now consumes a trailing `n`, keeps it in `raw`, parses the numeric value from the suffix-less literal, and emits an invalid-bigint diagnostic for decimal/exponent bigint forms.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible BigInt literal coverage for decimal, hex, binary, octal BigInts and invalid `1.2n`.

asi.hypothesis: BigInt literal scanning should compose with the numeric separator support already present for normal integer literals.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after BigInt suffix handling; separator stripping in integer parsing also works for decimal, hex, and binary BigInt literals.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible BigInt numeric separator coverage for `1_000n`, `0xff_ffn`, and `0b1010_0101n`.

asi.hypothesis: Export specifier parsing should support string-literal export names introduced in modern module syntax without changing import parsing.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; export specifier parsing already uses property-key parsing for both local and exported names, which accepts string literals.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export { value as "external-name" }` and string-named re-exports from another module.

asi.hypothesis: Class private-name parsing should support private identifiers in expression position for modern `#x in object` brand checks.
asi.learned: Adding a prefix parser branch for `#` lets PrivateIdentifier participate in normal binary parsing, including the existing `in` operator.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible private brand-check syntax coverage for `return #x in object` inside a class method.

asi.hypothesis: Existing for-in/for-of declaration parsing should cover const loop declarations in addition to var/let forms.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; loop declaration parsing preserves the `:const` kind for both for-in and for-of left-hand declarations.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible loop coverage for `for (const key in object)` and `for (const value of iterable)`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing switch parsing should preserve empty fallthrough cases and multi-statement consequents.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; switch case parsing emits empty consequent lists for fallthrough cases and accumulates statements until the next case/default label.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible switch fallthrough coverage with adjacent case labels, assignment update, break, and default assignment.

asi.hypothesis: Existing object expression spread parsing should cover object rest syntax in destructuring assignment-left contexts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; object rest assignment is represented with ObjectExpression plus SpreadElement on the assignment left-hand side.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible destructuring assignment coverage for `({ a, ...rest } = object)`.

asi.hypothesis: Catch binding parsing should compose with existing object/array binding patterns, including defaults and rest elements.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; catch clauses already call `parse_binding_pattern/1`, so destructuring catch params share variable/function parameter pattern support.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible catch binding coverage for object and array destructuring catch parameters.

asi.hypothesis: Existing super/member parsing should cover assignment to super properties and computed super properties inside class methods.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; `super` is parsed as an identifier-like primary and composes with member access on the left side of assignment expressions.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `super.value = value` and `super["other"] = value`.

asi.hypothesis: Existing rest parameter parser recovery should report errors when rest parameters are not final.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; the parameter parser expects `)` immediately after a rest binding, causing non-final rest parameter forms to return parser errors.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible syntax-error coverage for `function f(...rest, extra) {}` and `(...rest, extra) => rest`.

asi.hypothesis: Argument-list parsing should accept a trailing comma after a spread argument where supported by modern JavaScript parsers.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; the argument parser's spread branch already accepts comma followed by a closing parenthesis.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible spread-call coverage for `f(...args,)`.

asi.hypothesis: String escape scanning should implement JavaScript line continuations by omitting escaped line terminators from cooked string values.
asi.learned: Escaped line terminators are now consumed through the existing line-terminator handling and contribute an empty string to the cooked literal.
asi.quickjs_cases:
Adds QuickJS-compatible string literal coverage for a backslash-newline continuation in `"a\
b"` producing `ab`.

asi.hypothesis: The string line-continuation support should handle CRLF as a single escaped line terminator, not as two cooked characters.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after using `consume_line_terminator/1`; CRLF continuations are consumed as a single omitted line continuation.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible string literal coverage for a backslash-CRLF continuation.

asi.hypothesis: Lexer trivia handling should treat an initial `#!` line as a hashbang comment before normal tokenization.
asi.learned: Added hashbang skipping only at offset 0, reusing line-comment advancement semantics so later `#` remains available for private names and diagnostics.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible hashbang coverage for a script beginning with `#!/usr/bin/env quickjs`.

asi.hypothesis: String escape cooking should decode `\0` to a NUL byte instead of leaving it as the character `0`.
asi.learned: The lexer already had a NUL escape branch; the new focused QuickJS-derived coverage verifies the cooked value and compile checks caught an accidental duplicate branch during development.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible string literal coverage for `"a\0b"` producing a cooked NUL byte.

asi.hypothesis: Existing private-name class element parsing should support private methods and private method calls via member expressions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; private class keys are parsed by property-key parsing and `this.#method(value)` is handled by member/call expression parsing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `#method(value) {}` and `this.#method(value)`.

asi.hypothesis: Static class member parsing should combine with private method keys for modern class private static methods.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; static modifier handling feeds into the same private-key method parser used by instance private methods.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `static #method(value) {}` and a static caller method.

asi.hypothesis: Class accessor detection should recognize `get #name()` and `set #name(...)` as accessors rather than fields followed by methods.
asi.learned: Accessor lookahead now includes the private-name token pattern `# identifier (` alongside normal identifier and computed keys.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible private accessor coverage for `get #value()` and `set #value(value)`.

asi.hypothesis: Async method lookahead should recognize private method keys so `async #name()` is parsed as one async method.
asi.learned: Async method detection now includes private-name and async-generator private-name lookahead shapes in addition to identifier and computed keys.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `async #method(value) { return await value; }`.

description: Port async generator private method coverage

asi.hypothesis: Async generator private method parsing should be covered by the private-name async method lookahead added for the previous experiment.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in `mix test test/web_apis` due the known transient Process.monitor test returning `noproc` instead of `kaboom`; parser tests passed and the failure is unrelated, so retry the same experiment from a clean tree.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-add the focused async-generator private method parser test and rerun the experiment; if checks pass, keep it.
asi.quickjs_cases: Attempted QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `async *#method(value) { yield await value; }`.

description: Port async generator private method coverage

asi.hypothesis: Async generator private method parsing should be covered by the private-name async method lookahead added for async private methods.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after the async-method lookahead expansion; `async *#method` parses as an async generator MethodDefinition with a PrivateIdentifier key.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `async *#method(value) { yield await value; }` after retrying a transient web API check failure.

asi.hypothesis: Static modifier handling should compose with private accessor lookahead for `static get #x()` and `static set #x(v)`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after private accessor support; the class static modifier is consumed before the same accessor parser runs.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for static private getters and setters.

description: Port static async private method coverage

asi.hypothesis: Static method parsing should compose with async private method lookahead for `static async #name()`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; static modifier consumption leaves the parser positioned at the async private method start recognized in the previous lookahead fix.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `static async #method(value) { return await value; }`.

description: Port static async generator private method coverage

asi.hypothesis: Static modifier handling should also compose with async generator private method lookahead for `static async *#name()`.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after the async private generator lookahead branch; static members reuse the same async class method parser.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for `static async *#method(value) { yield await value; }`.

asi.hypothesis: Named export specifier parsing should treat contextual `default` as an exportable local name in re-export lists.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; export specifier key parsing accepts keyword tokens, so `default as namedDefault` is represented as an ExportSpecifier from a `default` identifier.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export { default as namedDefault } from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: Named import parsing should support the contextual `default as local` form in import specifier lists.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; import specifier parsing accepts keyword-like property names and preserves the imported/local identifier distinction.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `import { default as namedDefault } from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: Named import specifier parsing should accept string-literal imported names with local aliases.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in the known transient Process.monitor web API test (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`) while parser tests passed; the parser coverage change is unrelated and should be retried cleanly.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-add the string import-name test and rerun; keep if web API checks pass.
asi.quickjs_cases: Attempted module coverage for `import { "external-name" as localName } from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: Retrying the string import-name coverage should pass once the known Process.monitor timing flake does not occur.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed again in the same unrelated Process.monitor web API test (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`); parser tests still pass.
asi.next_action_hint: Defer or retry later after a clean web API run; avoid keeping while checks fail.
asi.quickjs_cases: Retried module coverage for `import { "external-name" as localName } from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: Named import specifier parsing should accept string-literal imported names with local aliases.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; import specifier parsing already uses property-key parsing for imported names, which accepts string literals.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `import { "external-name" as localName } from "dep"` after retrying transient web API flakes.

asi.hypothesis: Import specifier parsing should reject string literals as local binding names even when string imported names are accepted.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; after `as`, import parsing calls binding identifier parsing, which emits a diagnostic for a string literal local name.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module syntax-error coverage for `import { "external-name" as "local-name" } from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp literal scanning should not terminate on slash characters that appear inside a character class.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; regexp scanning already tracks character-class state and preserves flags in the regexp literal payload.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible regexp literal coverage for a character class containing `/` and `\]`, with `gi` flags.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp literal tokenization should preserve modern regexp syntax such as named capture groups and named backreferences without parser-level interpretation.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; regexp scanning treats the pattern body opaquely while respecting escapes and terminators, so named captures/backreferences are preserved in the literal payload.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible regexp literal coverage for `/(?<name>[a-z]+)\k<name>/u`.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp literal tokenization should preserve Unicode property escapes and flags without trying to parse the regexp grammar itself.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; escaped sequences remain part of the opaque regexp pattern and the `u` flag is preserved.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible regexp literal coverage for `/\p{Script=Greek}+/u`.

asi.hypothesis: Static import parsing should accept modern import attribute clauses after the module source for both normal and side-effect imports.
asi.learned: ImportDeclaration now carries optional attributes parsed as an object expression after `with { ... }` or `assert { ... }`; existing import declarations keep attributes nil.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `import data from "./data.json" with { type: "json" }` and side-effect import assertions.

asi.hypothesis: Module re-export parsing should accept import-attribute clauses after the source string for named and export-all declarations.
asi.learned: ExportNamedDeclaration and ExportAllDeclaration now carry optional attributes parsed through the same object-expression clause helper as imports.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for named re-export `with { type: "json" }` and export-all `assert { type: "json" }` attributes.

asi.hypothesis: Dynamic import parsing should naturally support an options object with import attributes as a second call argument.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; dynamic import is represented as a CallExpression and its second argument reuses normal object literal parsing, including `with` as a property key.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `import("./data.json", { with: { type: "json" } })`.

asi.hypothesis: Regexp/division lexical goal heuristics should treat `/` after identifiers, member expressions, and calls as division operators rather than regexp starts.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required for these cases; the lexer emits division punctuators after expression-ending tokens and Pratt parsing builds left-associative BinaryExpression nodes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible expression coverage for chained division, member-result division, and call-result division.

asi.hypothesis: Template literal parsing should compose with identifier and member-expression tags to represent tagged template expressions.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; postfix-tail parsing already recognizes template tokens after expressions and wraps them as TaggedTemplateExpression nodes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible tagged template coverage for identifier tags, member tags, and templates containing `${...}`.

asi.hypothesis: Numeric separator diagnostics should reject repeated, trailing, and prefix-adjacent separators in QuickJS-compatible numeric literals.
asi.learned: Validation already rejected repeated and trailing separators; added prefix-adjacent separator checks for binary/octal/hex numeric prefixes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible numeric separator diagnostics for `1__0`, `1_`, `0x_F`, and `0b_1`.

asi.hypothesis: BigInt literals should share numeric separator validation for repeated and prefix-adjacent separators.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after prefix-adjacent separator validation; BigInt raw literals include the suffix but still match repeated and prefixed separator diagnostics.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible BigInt separator diagnostics for `1__0n`, `0x_Fn`, and `0b_1n`.

description: Extend bigint separator diagnostics in same case

asi.hypothesis: Trailing numeric separators before a BigInt `n` suffix should be diagnosed like trailing separators on normal numeric literals.
asi.rollback_reason: The focused benchmark metric did not increase because this added an assertion to an existing QuickJS-port test module; preserve metric integrity by reverting and reintroducing as a separate focused case if still useful.
asi.next_action_hint: Add a distinct focused QuickJS-port test file for trailing BigInt separator diagnostics and keep it only if it increases coverage while checks pass.
asi.learned: Using a suffix-trimmed raw literal for separator edge checks fixes `1_n`; avoid bundling it into an existing counted case during autoresearch.

asi.hypothesis: A numeric separator immediately before the BigInt `n` suffix should be rejected as a trailing separator.
asi.learned: Separator validation now checks a suffix-trimmed literal so `1_n` is diagnosed while preserving the raw BigInt literal token for valid cases.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds a focused QuickJS-compatible diagnostic case for `value = 1_n`.

asi.hypothesis: Decimal numeric literals with missing exponent digits or separator immediately after exponent markers should be diagnosed.
asi.learned: Numeric validation now rejects decimal literals whose normalized raw text has `e`/`E` followed by an optional sign and then end-of-token or `_`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible numeric diagnostics for `1e`, `1e+`, `1e-`, and `1e_1`.

asi.hypothesis: Valid exponent numeric separators should parse in decimal literals after the first exponent digit.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in the known unrelated Process.monitor web API flake (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`) while parser tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-add the exponent separator coverage test and rerun; keep if checks pass.
asi.quickjs_cases: Attempted QuickJS-compatible numeric literal coverage for `1.5e1_0` and `1e+1_2`.

asi.hypothesis: Valid exponent numeric separators should parse in decimal literals after the first exponent digit.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; decimal scanning and number parsing already strip separators in exponent-bearing literals when separators are placed after exponent digits.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible numeric literal coverage for `1.5e1_0` and `1e+1_2` after retrying a transient web API flake.

asi.hypothesis: Prefixed numeric literals must contain at least one digit after `0x`, `0b`, or `0o`.
asi.learned: Numeric validation now rejects prefix-only normalized raw literals before parsing them to `:nan`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible diagnostics for missing digits in `0x`, `0b`, and `0o` literals.

asi.hypothesis: Prefixed numeric literal scanning should report invalid trailing identifier/digit characters that are not valid in the prefix radix.
asi.learned: After scanning a prefixed literal, validation now emits an invalid-number diagnostic if the next character is identifier-like, catching cases such as `0b2`, `0o8`, and `0xg`.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible prefixed numeric diagnostics for invalid binary, octal, and hexadecimal digits.

asi.hypothesis: Namespace re-export aliases should accept contextual export names such as `default`, not only binding identifiers.
asi.learned: Export-all alias parsing now uses property-key parsing after `as`, allowing keyword-style exported names while preserving existing identifier aliases.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export * as default from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: The export-all alias path should also accept string-literal exported module names where the grammar allows module export names.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required after switching export-all aliases to property-key parsing; string aliases are represented as Literal exported names.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module coverage for `export * as "external-name" from "dep"`.

asi.hypothesis: Namespace re-exports must require a `from` source clause whether their alias is an identifier or string module export name.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; export-all parsing expects `from` after the optional alias and reports a diagnostic if it is missing.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible module diagnostics for `export * as ns;` and `export * as "external-name";`.

asi.hypothesis: Object literal property-key parsing should accept numeric literal property names across decimal and prefixed forms.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; property-key parsing already accepts number tokens and preserves parsed numeric values for object keys.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible object literal coverage for numeric property names `0`, `1.5`, and `0x10`.

description: Port numeric class method key coverage

asi.hypothesis: Class element key parsing should accept numeric literal method names, including static methods with prefixed numeric names.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; class key parsing shares property-key parsing, so decimal and prefixed numeric keys become Literal method keys.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible class coverage for methods named `0`, `1.5`, and static `0x10`.

asi.hypothesis: Function and arrow formal parameter parsing should compose with object/array destructuring, defaults, and rest patterns.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; formal parameter parsing already delegates to binding-pattern parsing for both function declarations and parenthesized arrow parameters.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible function parameter coverage for object rest/default patterns, array rest patterns, and arrow destructuring defaults.

asi.hypothesis: Destructuring assignment-left parsing should preserve nested object and array destructuring shapes with defaults and rest elements.
asi.learned: No implementation change was required; nested object/array literal parsing already creates AssignmentPattern for default values and SpreadElement for rest-like assignment shapes.
asi.quickjs_cases: Adds QuickJS-compatible destructuring assignment coverage for nested object defaults and array rest elements.

asi.hypothesis: Variable declaration binding-pattern parsing should support nested object/array patterns with defaults and rest elements.
asi.rollback_reason: Backpressure failed in known unrelated Process.monitor flake (`noproc` instead of `kaboom`) while parser tests passed.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-add nested destructuring binding test and rerun; keep when web API checks pass.
asi.quickjs_cases: Attempted QuickJS-compatible binding coverage for nested object defaults and array rest in a const declaration.

asi.hypothesis: Variable declaration binding-pattern parsing should support nested object/array patterns with defaults and rest elements.
